raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need command to delete only in /system, not subdirs. Use find with -maxdepth 1. Or rm /system/*.txt but careful if none. Use rm -f /system/*.txt. But if glob expands to nothing, shell may error. Use shopt -s nullglob? But simplest: find /system -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.txt' -delete. Provide that.
